task_suite_audit: Independent implementations passed all 208 test methods. Eleven concurrency-sensitive suites passed 50 repeated runs each with zero failures.
model_note: The dated gpt-5.5-2026-04-23 snapshot was tested before scoring but is unsupported with Codex ChatGPT authentication, so this run uses the gpt-5.5 alias.

pilot.purpose: Infrastructure check only. Model outcomes cannot change the runner, arms, prompts, tests, or final sample.

pricing.note: This standard-rate API-equivalent estimate is not a Codex subscription bill. Codex reports aggregate session usage, so the estimate does not apply the per-prompt long-context surcharge.

amendments.change: Removed automatic long-context multipliers from the declared run.
amendments.reason: The official rule keys the surcharge to a prompt above 272K input tokens, while Codex exposes only aggregate session usage. Applying the threshold to an aggregate could overstate cost.

amendments.change: Treat a null optional long-context threshold as disabled in cost summarization and add a regression test.
amendments.reason: The 32-call infrastructure pilot completed grading but crashed during summary generation when the omitted threshold was represented as null. No arm, task, prompt, grader, score, or model setting changed.

victory_gate.accuracy: Sarathi pass rate is at least every other arm's pass rate.
victory_gate.cost: Sarathi estimated API cost per verified pass is at least 10 percent below every other arm.
victory_gate.noninferiority: The lower 95 percent interval for each Sarathi pass-rate difference is above -0.05.
victory_gate.infrastructure: No arm has an infrastructure-invalid result.

integrity_rules: Sarathi and competitor arms were frozen before task authoring.
Fresh task authors must not inspect any arm, earlier task suite, or benchmark result.
Independent reference implementations must pass every hidden assertion before model scoring.
The pilot may detect infrastructure defects only. Outcome-driven prompt, test, skill, or runner edits invalidate the final run.
Task prompts and hidden tests become public only after final scoring.
Skipped tests are infrastructure-invalid, never passes.
All raw outputs and candidate snapshots remain in ignored local artifacts.
